text.title: Accessibility Annotation Pattern Library Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying a11y-annotation-pattern-library; each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Library version says 1.0.0 across the design system but individual annotations carry inline overrides that contradict it.
antipattern.root-cause: Designers copy-edit snippets locally instead of evolving the library.
antipattern.fix: Treat the library as code: version-bump on every edit, and CI-block design files that drift from the current library version.

antipattern.symptom: Name pattern reads `Button` instead of imperative verb phrase like `Save changes`.
antipattern.root-cause: Designer used the archetype name as the literal label.
antipattern.fix: Annotation must instantiate the name_pattern with the actual user-facing label; CI-lint for placeholder labels.

antipattern.symptom: Dropdown filter annotated as `role=select` while behaving like a combobox with typeahead.
antipattern.root-cause: Designer picked the closest-sounding role without consulting APG.
antipattern.fix: Decision tree (06-decision-tree.xml) routes from behaviour to archetype to role; reject snippets where role disagrees with archetype.

antipattern.symptom: Modal opens and focus stays on the trigger button; Tab key escapes the modal to the page behind.
antipattern.root-cause: focus_behaviour annotation missing on the dialog archetype.
antipattern.fix: Pre-handoff audit script blocks design files where any `dialog` lacks the focus_behaviour annotation.
